content: No vendor publishes an exact Rev-N numbering spec. The closest documented analog is the South-Asian 'KOT' (Kitchen Order Ticket) convention (Petpooja/Gofrugal/Ivepos): the first fire produces a 'Fresh KOT', each subsequent fire produces a 'Repeat KOT' / 'Running KOT', each a separately-numbered ticket document linked to the same table/order; one final bill aggregates all KOTs for that table. This is a real, deployed pattern directly relevant given this product's Pakistan-first market.
